_purpose: Courthouse Atlas for Sullivan County, PA. The least populated county in the cluster (and one of the least in Pennsylvania). Formed from Lycoming County in 1847. The courthouse at Laporte — the smallest county seat in Pennsylvania — has not suffered a major fire. Records are thin because the population was always small.

current_courthouse.location: Laporte, PA — Main Street (population under 300 — the smallest county seat in Pennsylvania)

previous_courthouses.fate: County formed from Lycoming County in 1847. First permanent courthouse 1856.

record_losses.cause: No major courthouse fire or catastrophic loss
record_losses.description: Sullivan County's courthouse has not suffered a major fire. Records are continuous from the 1847 formation. Pre-1847 records are at Lycoming County in Williamsport. The county's small population means the total volume of records is limited — but what exists is intact. Lumber company land transactions dominate the deed records for much of the county's history.
record_losses.narrative_impact: Sullivan County's records are intact but thin — the population was always small, and the lumber workforce was transient. If {ancestor_name} lived in Sullivan County, the courthouse records may exist, but the person who worked the logging camps probably left no trace at the county seat.
record_losses.source: Sullivan County Prothonotary. Sullivan County Historical Society.

deeds.completeness: Good — continuous from county formation, though volume is small
deeds.gaps: Pre-1847 deeds at Lycoming County. Lumber company land dominated the deed books — individual small-holders were rare in the mountain areas.
deeds.repository: Sullivan County Recorder of Deeds, Laporte; PA State Archives microfilm

wills_estates.repository: Sullivan County Register of Wills; PA State Archives

marriages.completeness: Marriage licenses from 1885. Very small volume.
marriages.gaps: No civil licenses before 1885. Church records scarce due to small, scattered population.

court_minutes.repository: Sullivan County Prothonotary; PA State Archives

vital_records.completeness: PA vital registration from 1893. Compliance may have been spotty in remote mountain areas.
vital_records.repository: PA Division of Vital Records; PA State Archives

substitute_sources.description: The county historical society holds local history collections including logging-era photographs, lumber company records (fragmentary), and family files for the county's small population.
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: The historical society may be the only repository for lumber-era documentation. Family files for the county's founding families are held here.

substitute_sources.description: Eagles Mere, the mountaintop resort, maintained hotel registers, property records, and community association records that document the summer visitors and year-round residents.
substitute_sources.repository: Eagles Mere Museum and Historical Foundation
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: Documents the resort community — {ancestor_name} may appear as a seasonal worker or year-round resident.

narrative_hooks: Sullivan County's courthouse at Laporte is the smallest county seat in Pennsylvania — {ancestor_name}'s records, if they exist, are filed in a building that serves fewer than 6,000 people.
The lumber workforce that cut Sullivan County's forests was transient — if {ancestor_name} worked the logging camps, {pronoun_subject} may have left no trace at the Laporte courthouse.
Pre-1847 records for Sullivan County are at Lycoming County in Williamsport — the county boundary split whatever paper trail {ancestor_name}'s family had created.
Sullivan County's records are intact but thin — the population was never large, and the records reflect a county where more trees were counted than people.
source_references: Sullivan County Historical Society. Collections guide.
Taber, Thomas T. Ghost Lumber Towns of Central Pennsylvania. Williamsport: Lycoming County Historical Society, 1970.
PA Division of Vital Records. Guide to Pennsylvania Vital Records.
